Jesuse, ajínaño ñane iji baguíjnai ajei. Jeta ayorépise Jesuse iji jnumi udé ga
nóna ua iji baguíjnai, mu Jesuse nóna ua aja quedéjnane ome dáyipie, Dayé
Ujopie ore. Jecute Jesuse chojninga: “Ujetiga apota ga yijína ua aja baguíjnai
ajei.”*(Apocalipsis 3:20)*
english: At the same time, Jesus also appeared to one of his faithful believers named Ananias. He said to him, “Go to the center of the city and look for a man named Saul.”

ayoreo: Deréjnai tudé cojñone ore chiraja Dupade úringai pujnuéngaisṍri mu que ore changureta Jesuse go. Ore chucué dáyipiedie, ore chucué doré gaté iquetangóño cucha mu que ore changureta Dupade abi ome raque go. Chamáñaje ore déji erámi udé jogonórone cucha ajei te ga que ore chiraja dasocárique. Gusu Dupade ipotigasṍripísode uje ore chiraja dasocai. Gusu Dupade ipotigasṍripísode uje ore chiraja re iji Dupade adode ga ore chiraja rágome Dupade ga udojo ore u uje ore changureta Dupade Ujnacari Jesus uje toi ome ore.*(Santiago 2:19,20)*
english: Thomas was not with the other disciples when Jesus appeared to them, but they told him that their Lord was alive again. But Thomas didn’t believe what they told him, and he said: “I have my doubts. If I don’t see the prints of the nails in the palms of his hands, I can’t believe he’s alive.”

ayoreo: Jé uacamóño ayorérique a ca chujé dojórique ayoré, que pésu cucha gajnarétique, mu que changureta Dupade. Dupade que jno údi go. Jecute uqué cojñoque ugucharãi chirase dáringai ugucharãque. Que chiraja Dupade uruode yúrique go. Mu chise gójni diri tudé enga ujetiga chicai gajño pioi te ga, cho jóroque dáyugu re, jnusina jóroque dáringai cojñoque ugucharãc. Jnusina jóroque dáringai ayoré subudé iji jnumi uje que chimo chaguhei ome re nanique, mu que toc jeta qué chisapo ayoré ijõre iji, aníri, iji pitoringai ejoi.
english: But the young man loved being well off.  He loved being rich, more than he loved*Dupade*.

ayoreo: Yimóji*Chícode*gari mu yajéngopis ome*Chícode*uje que catecã́ri yu. A yiyápo*Chícode*rĩ uje que catecã́ri yu ga yibágui*Ecarai*. Jé yimesẽre*Ecarai*uje jĩrique catecã́ri yu, mu chi*Chícode*chijnóra yu diríca. Que catecã́ri yu diríca mu chisapo yu rĩ ga chojninga: “Qué yajéngome ua diríca gu.”
english: I slept with*Chícode,*but I was bashful around him because he didn’t talk to me. So I broke up with him, because he didn’t talk to me, and I went to*Ecarai.*I liked*Ecarai*because he quickly started talking to me, but he told me that*Chícode*loved me.

ayoreo:
Mu yojninga: “Úngare. Dupade que
ajéo u go jetiga chujépise dojo ayoré,” yojninga. Carajnoque ore pojna yu uje
yaquĩ́ra udire ájninganenie.
english: And so Eruidaté left. She said: “I’ll go to her.” And she was gone.

ayoreo: Tími, chi ayoréode chi yocãrapíode chi jec u cuchisode udore iji erámi to nanique ome jnani bajade. Ẽjé. Cuchiso ponáyone. Chi yocãrapíode u to ome jnacare bajade. Mu que ore chiraja gu. Chiese yocãrapíode cuchiso  ponáyone iji erámi to iji uté, jnacare bajade. Ẽjé. Cuchisode jnese. Chiese yocãrapíode jé u to ome jnacare bajade.
english: They told about the tapir taking an Ayoré man away with him, one of the first men told about this. The Ayoré crawled upon him one day. And the tapir provided all the different kinds of food for him. He carried the Ayoré. And he gave the Ayoré food things. The Ayoré got hungry and that is when he gave him honey to eat. And told him how to get honey. And how to chop for it. And how to suck it. The tapir said, “Why, honey is really delicious.’ And the honey gourd was filled with honey. He said he took it out of the honeycomb.

ayoreo: Cucha dacadode jetiga Satanás atígo sué yoquibai gajnerĩ, yicoi ga yatecãcoi Dupade gajnerĩ. Yingongome gajnerĩ jeta yanguretágo ga Dupade chijócãrape gajnerĩ iji yocajéode. Ga que chisapo yoque rĩ, Satanás. Yirajago uje Satanás u uje pésu ayoréode yocujuredie ome cuchade. Cuchade jnese. Chingongara cuchade aja yoque iji jnumi tudé jeta ipótago ga ca yangácoi Dupade ome guerãtígo cucha.  Satanás ujopierãque to.  Satanás u, mu Dupade u uje ujopie sué gai. A jé areque gajnerĩ ujetiga yicoi iroquéode gajnerĩ. Jé ca atique gajnaréngome yoque iji deréjnane udojo, Dupade.
english: This isn’t*Dupade*’s will for us at all. His will is for us to never stop thinking about him. He wants us to do what he wills. Let’s not allow our minds to leave him, for*Dupade*is the important one these days.

ayoreo:
Dupade chise ore. Dupade
chajmita ore iji guedéjnoringai uté, mu chi ore pógu raque iji quedéjnarique.
english: God came to them in the late afternoon, but they had hidden themselves.

ayoreo: *Pujnuiné – Tobité,Bolivia – 1960*
english: *Pujnuiné– Tobité, Bolivia – 1965.*

ayoreo: Qué yedobu jnacarode acoté*Chequé Dago*. Chojninga: Ẽ́ yisapié yacoté gapu.*Pujnucode*déji sañeque ga tangári yu ga chirate yu. Ca chajéngome yu jne. Chiradirase yu iji dejode gajnerĩ, yacoté casé,*Átapide*.
english: I’m going to sing about*Chequé Dago*who was the wife of young men. Supposedly, I had relations with a young girl. When*Pujnúcode*was away he heard me sing and imitated me. He didn’t respect me. He tried to imitate what I sang about a former wife called Átapide.

ayoreo:
A jé ca yujóraque chequé oyo yu
gotique, uje yetaguipise sigásodie uje ore chijna. Yitodopísa borácho yugode
rĩ. Que yipota jetiga uñeque idógosi iji yu.
english: I’m happy, because I believe in Dupade with all my heart. But this is what I am not happy about, my husband doesn’t believe in Dupade. It would be so much better if all my family were sincerely following Dupade – my husband, my children, and myself.

ayoreo: Cojñócho ayoréode. Chi ayoré Aramato, cho otadábode. Ore chujé Yamochai ga chijaro ajnoquéode ga chisite ajnoquéode. Ga pésu ayoréode adode tiji jnese, Aramato. Pésu ayoré; chijaro ajnoquéode, Yamochai ajnoquéode. Chimate ga chibote ujñari aja ajei. Chimate ga ayoré. Chi chicháji ti. Chiese cojñone yocarãpi tu Yamochai ajnoquéode.
english: Moon went down another road. Moon was first to arrive, and he caused the Ayoré people to stay dead. “Ayoré people were like the non-Ayoré people at first,” the father told his little boy. “They killed *Yamochai (Monkey) and they ground his bones and sifted them into powder. “Lizard was an Ayoré before, and had legs. And Lizard took the powder made from the ground-up Monkey’s bones and made body parts from it. She finished and then she blew breath into them. When she finished doing this, it was a person. “It’s said that she put the first man to one side, and started to make the rest. “They say that non-Ayoré people and Ayoreos all were made from the Monkey’s bones.” The father said to his little son, “Animals originated from a very large woman. Bigger than anything.”

ayoreo: Ore edópasa*pajojo.*Picanerane ore edópasa tu*sioti*te rĩ. Uje ore ayo nanique uje chósi te iji ore enochai enga Picanerane edópasa. Picanerane edópasa*cuquébi*(un tipo de pájaro). (Oidabiadé cho quique: Ayoré nanique ga jeta baro*Patricia*potarása*Ayoré Gajnui*ga uyu ga yiji – mu*Noemia*abai tu ga yãratayipis ome*Juan*uhuaque enga dire uñai enga*Patricia*cha. Mu chugúpẽre uté u iji datí. Boquere chugúpẽre. Datedie ore chojningame dabode: “Aya dara, “*cuquébi a!*” Ujetiga abi que cha go enga daté chojninga: “¡*Cuquep*!”) Yoquedópasa*coyidé*(un tipo de pájaro) uje chojninga: “*Coyi-coyi-coyi*!” Agoyé u. Que yiraja ore edópasade, cucherane jnoji yáyipie gu. Jnuriminone ore edópasa pitoringai uje yitogoi. Chi ore edópasa pitoringai. Mu que yiraja Jnuriminone edopasade to. Ujeta gosi acoté toi ga abai chojninga: “Yimope cochocoi abubie sogójma yacoté uaqué to que.” Gaidi u uje chijnorapise dacoté ga chijna edópasa ingoningájnai taja. *Oidabiade – Campo Loro, Paraguay – 1988.* *Marina de Morarie churúsãre Oidabiadé atatagode udojo.*
english: Ayoré warriors when they worked themselves up to kill long ago would liken themselves to the armadillos, that’s because the armadillo was a*weapon carrier*in far off times and would dig into the ground to protect himself. They say, it was the*Chiquenone*clan possession in far off times and that’s why they would liken themselves to it.

ayoreo: Jé adi isocai. Chojninga: “Que yĩrarare go.”
english: *Adudui*said, “I didn’t say anything.” He wanted to hide from the tapir that he was hungry.

ayoreo: Dupade a jé iroque yu aja de ujetiga yicáji udé ga jé ca cuchárique chosi tiji yu. Yacaranguipise rĩ uje yacoté e aregue uje yatecãraitigade u gu. Dupade chapúsa dasocade ome yu ome cucha dacadode udojo uje yejna yiguesi ome Dupade uje yicatecã́ri.
english: I’m so grateful that I can be here in Cochabamba with fellow-believers in*Dupade*like you. But what I’m happy about is that I haven’t forgotten*Dupade*. That I haven’t put him out of my mind or my heart. I thank*Dupade*that he brought me to Cochabamba so that I could rejoice with you in His Son Jesus Christ.

ayoreo: *Marina de Morarie churúsãre Guibéjnané atatagode udojo.*
english: That was the place I would have been at the salt beds, right where they were coming. But I looked and saw*Garáijnaidái*over there. He said: “Let’s go! I’ll leave our stuff over there.”

ayoreo:
Uje yirajágo
gu uje quigode cuse. Quigode u uje, ujetiga yipótago puru uñeque ga yangácoi.
Ga jé u uje, aníri, yirajágo uje pacajnaquéode déji gu. Enga jé areguipise iji
dirode udojo gajnerĩ uje yirajágo uje yacágo aja ñane ajéode gu, cojñone,
ayoréode ore uyóguiji erámi jnese iji dirode udojo gajnerĩ.
english: It’s true that, even when we are miles apart in understanding each other,*Dupade*knows what is in our hearts. He knows all about our sins.  And he knows what we think about His Son, and whether our thoughts are good. For*Dupade*isn’t far off, he’s right here beside us wherever we are in this world. The end.

ayoreo: Mu jecucha ajnusínaño*Jesucrísto i*, uje ueradéjnapise gu. Pujopié déji i to, ga jé cho Pablo urusarane.*(Filipenses 2:9-11)*
english: But always remember that the name of Jesus is very beautiful. And remember that there is power in his name.*(Philippians 2:9-11)*

ayoreo:
Uje cucha pibosode u yocade ore.
Yacorenie chiraja uje yujégo enga ore chayome yoque ga que ore diguiji yocajei
go. Jé basa ga a que yipéco cucha gajnarétique tiji ome Dupade nanique, enga
yacorenie, yocade diguiji yocajei enga yajo. Enga ayoré casodicaite gosi
báguiji erámi. Que chimo cuténique míraque ga chaguhéode ujuya ga cho jõ. Cho
caru ome erámi isagode.
english: But*Dupade*wasn’t through with them at all, and he had a plan for them, a way for them to return to his country. He said: “Even if you didn’t have to leave,” he said, “you wouldn’t be permitted to eat freely without working.”

ayoreo:
Mu Jesute que uruode u gotique jeta chojninga,
‘Yapade, yiji ome ore yocurasade iji jnumi, mu yiyaque ore jnese,’ que
chojninga go. Jesus uruode u ga chojningame udore: “Yapade, yiji to ome ore uje
yejna baroquéode guesi, mu arodipise ore jne iji jnumi.”
english: But Jesus told the truth about what*Dupade*’s Word says: when believers give their all to preach*Dupade*’s Word, they will not be sad when*Dupade*comes back. And that is how it was with Jesus, for he said: “We haven’t yet lost any of those that you gave to me here on earth, but only that one that your Word said would be lost, just that one.”

ayoreo:
Mu gusu udore uje que ore changureta
ga ore todo yui, uje ore todo yui ujetiga pujuya ore ome pioi, jeoga pujuyaquéo
uña.  Mu Dupade ujuyaquei tu uje tṍraji
gaté jne. Que yirajágo ujetiga yodi upo rĩ, ujeta pioque. Mu yirajágopo rĩ
uje pioque a u jne, ejórique, jeta cho dajuñame jnumi jogosórone uyoque.
english: **nly****Jesus Christ****can forgive our sins****:**I wanted you to hear these words of mine,*Señora Marina*, because today we have repented with all out hearts. And we’re following Jesus for only he can forgive our sins, and it is he that rose again to give new life to his believers. He did that so that when he returns to earth for us, we will go up to meet him in the air.

ayoreo:
Enga yirajágo uje a bajéngome
cuchade gu uje pisijnaringuei chisa ua enga e bajéngome cuchade. Chi ore chojninga: “Ugúcoi ne,”
chojninga. Chi chojninga: “Ugúcoi ne mu yacóco yoque que uje choquíbi yoque
gu.” Mu chojninga: “Ise ga e acho
yarengué cucha a. Jecute uacapócho uaque,” chojninga. Mu chojningame Dupade: “Dupade,
e yagu. Berané chequé chisiome yu ga yagu to,” chojninga, “Uje chojninga, Adã,
agu to.” Mu jé ũrepise ujetiga bacoté ca
changureta Dupade enga a jé ca áta ua aja ipesutígo cucha gajnarécho ujeta
angureta Dupade. Bacoté ujetiga ca changureta Dupade enga jé ca arate uaté aja
ipesutígo cucha gajnarécho.
english: So, they said: “Well, here is*Dupade*!”

ayoreo: Yisago yocugúchade. Yico garai ga yimóngo iji ta uje guedé jno. E dejac uje yimóngo iji yoquirengúi. Chaguhei chejnapise yoque. Jnanione chachẽre dati múñi que yajo cuchárique. Yico garai údi mu ore chojninga: “*Burai*ducoré chacose á.”
english: We grabbed our things. We went toward the plains and saw that the sun was going down. By the time we caught sight of our camp it was night. We were hungry. Even though the men had been scouting for food we hadn’t eaten anything. We crossed the plains, and they told us: “*Burai*and them are hiding.”

ayoreo: Yijñecarósi ji de.
english: *Pejnócode chedo dajnusiétigade ome dacotedie*

ayoreo: *Uneredaté*chojninga: “*Edoguesaide*, tágape ua. Que jnacanique etácho yu gotique.”
english: She said to her children: “Stay here. I’m going to*Guidábi*. I’m going directly there. ¿Could a young man refuse me?”

ayoreo: – ore uniri u*G**arai*nanique. (ore i ejoi tu) – ore uniri i tu*C**ucójna*bajaque, ejoi i tu*J**nupedo**Cucarãte**Cuchábode*ga jnupedo déji.
english: Their land was in the plains where there were many*bottle trees*. *Jnupedógosode –*“People of the caves.

ayoreo:
Ga unojo u uje Dupade cho daru
ome jnani bai ga uruode unojo uje chojninga: “A gajnetui yore babai,”
chojninga, “quigade eti baque to,” chojninga. Enga cho daru aja anire, yocãrapié chequé bajé. “Iraja,” chojninga, “uje
báyipie, bajei gaidode a babai u,” chojninga, “Bapotiguéode gajnengóri tu
babai,” chojningame. “Mu a uacatígo cuse jne,” chojninga. E cho darú ome yocaté chequé
bajé to. “A uacatique jne, mu qué baraja ũrosoringuéone,” chojninga, “Baraja
ũrosoringuéone enga que toc ujeta batique tocádiji ua bisideque,” chojninga. Ga unojo uje yirajágo uje Dupade
guerãtique cucha gajnaréngone ome yocayode jnani bajade uje cho daru aja ore. Quigade ga cho daru aja erámi. Chojningame erámi: “A bojnatígo cuse jne,” chojninga. Dupade guejnai chi gusu anírone nanique, acade cucha, cucha a. Que cucha ojnatique déji to. Mu catáguedie déji ayoréode yoquejnane. Mu Dupade cho daru aja jnumi ga chojninga: “A bojnatígo cuse jne,” chojninga. “A ojnacúco,” chojninga. Ga quigade uacamóño uje que
ayorérique guejnatique déji siñeque jeta ca catágueraque déji. A ca déji
siñeque jetiga ca ojnacúcoraque déji. Ojnacúcodie déji erámi jnese uje Dupade
cho daru aja erámi.
english: But now he cursed the first man with death, and that is why we all die, because*Dupade*said we would.

ayoreo: **chatata uje jnani bajade que ore chiraja uje Dupade u uje déji yoquibai:**
english: He’d call out also for pools of water. And instead of rain, without knowing why,*Dupade*would place a jaguar in their path. They would kill the jaguar, for that is what*Dupade*provided, so that is what they killed. For*Dupade*was always placing things in front of them because they called upon him long ago.

ayoreo:
A yisapo Rincón dirome rĩ jeta
e Dijaide doi enga chicáji ome congreso. 
A yicopo Señora Marina dirome rĩ. Yirasique uje ore chudute yuruode. Que
toque go jetiga jé yajnésãra yiquigade. Que toque go jetiga yudute ayoréraque
urúrique ga que toguipise gu.
english: But, I’m not lying about that. It makes me so happy because he does teach our family about*Dupade*’s*Word.*And that is why I never want to turn back from where I now am. I have found myself at last, and I don’t ever want to live the way I did. Especially now, in these days since I have given myself to*Dupade*. And especially now that we have a testimony to maintain before others. There are some women who complain about*Dupade*’s*Word*, but I don’t complain about anything to do with*Dupade*.

ayoreo:
Dupade*Ujopié*chijna danguresórone aja ñane ga cho jetiga ore chojmára iji
jnumi enga uje ore jnome gatajnoquei ga ore jno jnese.*(Romanos 12:4-5)*
english: On the other hand, if we don’t all believe in*Dupade*, some of us will be punished by*Dupade*.*(John 3:36)*
